jd_text: Software engineer position at a tech company. Requires Python and React.
report:
Running startup checks and reading the evaluation mode so the report matches career-ops format.
Running startup checks and reading the evaluation mode so the report matches career-ops format.
## Evaluation: Software Engineer (unnamed company)

**Input type:** JD text only — no URL, no company name, no location. Liveness cannot be verified.

---

### Block A — Role Summary

| Field | Assessment |
|--------|------------|
| **Archetype** | **Unclassifiable / generalist full-stack** — too little signal for AI Platform, Agentic, PM, SA, FDE, or Transformation. Closest hybrid: **AI Forward Deployed** (build + ship) if the stack implies product delivery, but the JD does not say so. |
| **Domain** | Unknown — “tech company” only |
| **Function** | Build (inferred from “software engineer” + stack) |
| **Seniority** | Not stated |
| **Remote** | Not stated |
| **Team size** | Not stated |
| **TL;DR** | Generic full-stack role asking for Python and React, with no company, scope, level, or location — insufficient to target an application. |

---

### Block B — Match with CV

| JD requirement | CV evidence | Match |
|----------------|-------------|--------|
| **Python** | Skills: “**Python (FastAPI)**”; Career Ops Dashboard: “**FastAPI backend**”; infra: Docker, nginx, Tailscale | **Partial** — real but secondary; primary career is frontend/e-commerce |
| **React** | Skills: “**React 19**”; Spotshop Transporter PWA: “**React 19 + Vite + Tailwind CSS**”; 8+ years frontend | **Strong** |
| **Software engineer (general)** | 8+ years production; Bewise widgets, PWAs, Shopify themes, full-stack side projects | **Strong on delivery**, **unclear on backend depth** |

**Strengths (from CV)**

- React is core production work, not a side skill — Spotshop PWA, widget suite, modern stack.
- Python appears in shipped-adjacent work: Career Ops Dashboard (Astro + **FastAPI**).
- Full-stack posture when needed: frontend owns UI; integrates with C# REST backend on Spotshop; deploys own infra (VPS, nginx, Docker).

**Gaps**

| Gap | Blocker? | Mitigation |
|-----|----------|------------|
| **Python as primary language** | **Unknown** — could be blocker if role is backend-heavy | Lead with FastAPI + Career Ops; frame Python as “when the product needs a thin API layer” |
| **No AI/UI angle in JD** | Soft | Your AI work (Plugly, streaming UIs, agent dashboards) may be irrelevant or a differentiator — JD doesn’t say |
| **Company/domain unknown** | Hard for tailoring | Cannot customize until you have a real posting |
| **Seniority undefined** | Medium | Default to mid–senior frontend with growing full-stack; ask level in first contact |

---

### Block C — Level & Strategy

**Level detected:** Not stated — could be mid, senior, or full-stack generalist.

**Your natural level (for this vague JD):** Senior frontend / mid full-stack — 8+ years production UI, Python on supporting projects, not years of backend-first Python.

**Sell senior without overstating**

- “**8 years building real webshops and PWAs** that serve real users and generate real revenue.”
- “**Built the full frontend**” on Spotshop; integrated with order APIs and payment flows.
- “**Full-stack job search dashboard: Astro + FastAPI backend**, deployed via nginx + Tailscale.”

**If they downlevel**

- Accept only if comp matches senior frontend market and Python is a minority of the role.
- Negotiate a 6-month review if the job is >50% Python backend.
- Clarify: product engineering vs platform/backend team.

**Positioning:** Frontend engineer who ships full-stack when needed — not a Python-first backend engineer unless the real JD says otherwise.

---

### Block D — Comp & Demand

| Item | Finding |
|------|---------|
| **JD compensation** | Not mentioned |
| **Company comp reputation** | Cannot research — no company name |
| **Market demand (Python + React)** | Broad stack; common in startups and product teams; no posting-specific signal |
| **Denmark / remote** | Not stated; you’re in Hjørring — location policy unknown |

**Note:** No web comp lookup was run. Treat any number as unknown until you have company + location + seniority.

---

### Block E — Customization Plan

*Premature for this stub — apply after you have a real posting. Directional plan if the full JD is “React UI + Python API”:*

| # | Section | Current | Proposed change | Why |
|---|---------|---------|-----------------|-----|
| 1 | Headline | “AI Frontend Engineer” | “Frontend Engineer · React · Python (FastAPI)” | Mirrors stated stack without hiding AI |
| 2 | Summary | E-commerce + AI emphasis | Add one line: full-stack delivery (React UI + FastAPI APIs) | Matches dual stack |
| 3 | Skills order | Frontend first | Group “**React 19**” and “**Python (FastAPI)**” at top | ATS alignment |
| 4 | Experience | Spotshop under Bewise | Lead bullet: React 19 PWA + API integration | Direct React + integration proof |
| 5 | Side projects | Career Ops listed | Elevate: “10-page dashboard, FastAPI backend, production deploy” | Only explicit Python product |

**Top 5 CV tweaks:** headline, summary line on FastAPI, reorder skills, Spotshop bullet up, Career Ops bullet expanded.

**Top 5 LinkedIn tweaks:** same headline; Featured link to Career Ops or Spotshop; About paragraph on React + FastAPI; skills endorsements React/Python; experience dates aligned with CV.

---

### Block F — Interview Plan

| # | JD requirement | STAR+R story | S | T | A | R | Reflection |
|---|----------------|--------------|---|---|---|---|------------|
| 1 | React | Spotshop Transporter PWA | Driver pickup/delivery tool needed | Ship reliable mobile UI fast | React 19 + Vite + Tailwind; integrated with order API via C# backend | Shipped driver-facing PWA | Frontend ownership + API integration beats “I know React” |
| 2 | React (scale) | Bewise Plus widgets | Multiple DanDomain shops | Reusable widgets across clients | Smarty + JsRender; JSON API with large catalogs | Widgets on live SMB shops | Platform constraints teach reusable UI patterns |
| 3 | Python | Career Ops Dashboard | Personal job-search tooling | Full product, not a toy | Astro + Tailwind v4 + **FastAPI**; nginx + Tailscale deploy | 10 functional pages, real eval flow | Shows you can own API layer when needed |
| 4 | Python (AI path) | Plugly AI Chat | Shop assistant for DanDomain | Shop-agnostic widget | DOM context, `/json/products`, Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter | Dev-stage product with multi-model backend | AI + commerce if role touches product |
| 5 | Software engineer (delivery) | Agency e-commerce (2018–2021) | Tight deadlines, real clients | Accessible shops under pressure | Responsive builds on DanDomain | Production shops for Danish SMBs | Agency pace = shipping discipline |
| 6 | Integration | Spotshop + backend split | You frontend, other dev backend | Clean handoff | Order lookup, payment capture, status via REST | Working end-to-end flow | Good answer if they probe “full stack” |

**Recommended case study:** **Spotshop Transporter PWA** — React 19 in production, real users, API integration; add **Career Ops** if they stress Python.

**Red-flag questions**

- *“Are you a backend Python engineer?”* — “Primary strength is React production UIs; I’ve built and deployed **FastAPI** backends for my own products when the team needs a thin API layer.”
- *“Why leave e-commerce?”* — Only if the real company isn’t e-commerce; otherwise lean into domain depth.
- *“Years of Python?”* — Honest: production Python on side/full-stack projects; depth is FastAPI services, not years of Django monoliths.

---

### Block G — Posting Legitimacy

**Assessment tier: Proceed with Caution** (leaning **Suspicious** until a real posting exists)

This is not a verifiable job posting — it’s a two-line stub with no company, URL, date, or apply path.

| Signal | Finding | Weight |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Posting freshness | No URL / no date | **Concerning** |
| Apply path | None | **Concerning** |
| Description quality | Only “Python and React”; no scope, team, level, comp | **Concerning** |
| Company hiring signals | Cannot search — no company name | **Neutral** |
| Reposting / scan history | N/A | **Neutral** |
| Role–company fit | Unknown | **Neutral** |

**Context:** Could be a placeholder, recruiter teaser, or test prompt — not something to invest a tailored CV/PDF in until you have company name, full JD, and URL (then run `node check-liveness.mjs <url>`).

---

## Verdict

**Technical fit on the two stated skills:** React is a strong match; Python is a credible but secondary match (FastAPI on Career Ops, not a Python-first career).

**Practical fit:** Poor — you cannot apply, research comp, or verify the role. Do not spend application effort on this text alone.

**Score:** **2.8/5**

| Dimension | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|
| CV match | ~3.5 — React strong, Python partial |
| North Star | ~2.5 — generic SWE, no AI/frontend identity in JD |
| Comp | N/A — drags score down |
| Culture / remote | N/A |
| Red flags | No company, no URL, no scope |

**Legitimacy:** **Proceed with Caution**

---

**Recommendation:** Do not apply to this stub. Paste the **full JD or URL** (and company name if you have it) for a proper A–G report, liveness check, and tracker entry.
